task_completion.description: Navigate to the Apps section in the device Settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Android Settings app is open and the 'Apps' or 'All apps' menu is displayed.

task_completion.description: Locate the Flight Crew View app in the installed apps list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Crew View' app is visible in the list of installed applications or search results.

task_completion.description: Open the App info page for Flight Crew View.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'App info' screen is displayed with 'Flight Crew View' and its icon at the top.

task_completion.description: Access the battery usage settings for the app.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Battery' or 'App battery usage' settings page specific to Flight Crew View is displayed.
The 'Unrestricted' radio button or toggle is selected on the battery settings page for Flight Crew View.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Android graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Android Settings app GUI, not a terminal, command line, or ADB shell.

integrity.description: Verify the settings changes were applied to the correct application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The App info and Battery settings screens explicitly show the name 'Flight Crew View' or package 'com.robert.fcView'.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction occurred in the genuine Android Settings application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Android Settings app, not a text editor, browser, or mock application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target package is 'com.robert.fcView' (Flight Crew View). The expected state is 'unrestricted', which is the standard Android terminology for exempting apps from device idle/doze mode restrictions. The pass threshold is 50 points.
